title: ds-viz-column - option matrix
description: Test bench for splunk.column. Each panel exercises a distinct option group: stack modes, columnGrouping, columnSpacing/seriesSpacing, dataValuesDisplay, xAxisLabelRotation, annotations, dual y-axis, split sub-charts, sparkbar pattern. SPL is makeresults-based so this dashboard runs in any Splunk instance with no setup. Same axis convention as line/area: hide x-axis title (the word _time / category-name is noise when the title repeats it), keep labels visible (auto), hide major tick marks.

options.query: | makeresults count=8 | streamstats count as i | eval category = case(i=1,"Auth",i=2,"Search",i=3,"API",i=4,"Web",i=5,"DB",i=6,"Cache",i=7,"Queue",1==1,"Storage"), requests = round(800 - (i*50) + random()%200) | table category requests

| makeresults count=7 | streamstats count as i | eval region = case(i=1,"Mon",i=2,"Tue",i=3,"Wed",i=4,"Thu",i=5,"Fri",i=6,"Sat",1==1,"Sun"), us_east = round(800 + 200*sin(i/2) + random()%100), us_west = round(600 + 250*sin(i/3+1) + random()%100), eu_west = round(450 + 150*sin(i/2+2) + random()%80) | table region us_east us_west eu_west

| makeresults count=7 | streamstats count as i | eval day = case(i=1,"Mon",i=2,"Tue",i=3,"Wed",i=4,"Thu",i=5,"Fri",i=6,"Sat",1==1,"Sun"), frontend = round(40 + 15*sin(i/2) + random()%8), backend = round(60 + 20*sin(i/3+1) + random()%10), database = round(30 + 12*sin(i/2+2) + random()%6) | table day frontend backend database

| makeresults count=7 | streamstats count as i | eval day = case(i=1,"Mon",i=2,"Tue",i=3,"Wed",i=4,"Thu",i=5,"Fri",i=6,"Sat",1==1,"Sun"), mobile = round(35 + 10*sin(i/2)), desktop = round(45 + 8*sin(i/3+1)), tablet = round(20 + 5*sin(i/2+2)) | table day mobile desktop tablet

| makeresults count=7 | streamstats count as i | eval day = case(i=1,"Mon",i=2,"Tue",i=3,"Wed",i=4,"Thu",i=5,"Fri",i=6,"Sat",1==1,"Sun"), target = 100, actual = round(60 + 35*sin(i/2) + random()%15) | table day target actual

| makeresults count=8 | streamstats count as i | eval product = case(i=1,"Phones",i=2,"Tablets",i=3,"Laptops",i=4,"TVs",i=5,"Audio",i=6,"Wearables",i=7,"Cameras",1==1,"Gaming"), delta_pct = round((random()%4000-2000)/100, 1) | table product delta_pct
| makeresults count=10 | streamstats count as i | eval bucket = "Bin " . i, count = round(50 + 80*sin(i/2) + random()%30) | table bucket count

| makeresults count=8 | streamstats count as i | eval product_name = case(i=1,"Premium Wireless Headphones",i=2,"4K Ultra HD Television 55-inch",i=3,"Ergonomic Office Chair",i=4,"Stainless Steel Water Bottle",i=5,"Mechanical Gaming Keyboard",i=6,"Bluetooth Portable Speaker",i=7,"Smart Home Security Camera",1==1,"Wireless Charging Pad"), revenue = round(1000 + 800*sin(i/2) + random()%400) | table product_name revenue

| makeresults count=12 | streamstats count as i | eval month = case(i=1,"Jan",i=2,"Feb",i=3,"Mar",i=4,"Apr",i=5,"May",i=6,"Jun",i=7,"Jul",i=8,"Aug",i=9,"Sep",i=10,"Oct",i=11,"Nov",1==1,"Dec"), revenue = round(100000 + 30000*sin(i/3) + (i*5000) + random()%10000) | table month revenue

| makeresults count=2 | streamstats count as i | eval annotationX = case(i=1,"Mar",1==1,"Nov"), annotationLabel = case(i=1,"Product Launch",1==1,"Black Friday"), annotationColor = case(i=1,"#FFB627",1==1,"#FF2D95") | table annotationX annotationLabel annotationColor

ds_dualaxis.name: dual axis - volume column and conversion line overlay

options.query: | makeresults count=7 | streamstats count as i | eval day = case(i=1,"Mon",i=2,"Tue",i=3,"Wed",i=4,"Thu",i=5,"Fri",i=6,"Sat",1==1,"Sun"), visits = round(8000 + 2000*sin(i/2) + random()%1000), conversion_pct = round(2 + 1.5*sin(i/3+1) + random()%1*0.1, 1) | table day visits conversion_pct

ds_split.name: split series - small multiples by metric

options.query: | makeresults count=7 | streamstats count as i | eval day = case(i=1,"Mon",i=2,"Tue",i=3,"Wed",i=4,"Thu",i=5,"Fri",i=6,"Sat",1==1,"Sun"), cpu_pct = round(40 + 30*sin(i/2) + random()%10), memory_gb = round(8 + 4*sin(i/3+1) + random()%2*0.1, 1), disk_iops = round(2000 + 1500*sin(i/2+2) + random()%500) | table day cpu_pct memory_gb disk_iops
| makeresults count=20 | streamstats count as i | eval bin = "b" . i, value = round(50 + 30*sin(i/2) + random()%10) | table bin value

options.markdown:
# `splunk.column` - option matrix

Vertical bars - **categorical x-axis, value y-axis**. Each panel exercises one option group. **Convention:** `xAxisTitleVisibility:"hide"` (avoid repeating the category name), `xAxisLabelVisibility:"auto"`, `xAxisMajorTickVisibility:"hide"`. **Column-specific:** `columnGrouping` (`auto`/`overlay`), `columnSpacing`, `seriesSpacing`, `xAxisLabelRotation` (-90/-45/0/45/90), `dataValuesDisplay` (off/all/minmax). Annotations are supported.

viz_basic.title: 1. Minimal - single series
viz_basic.description: Bare options applied. Default columnGrouping=auto, default seriesSpacing.

viz_unstacked.title: 2. Multi-series side-by-side - default auto grouping
viz_unstacked.description: stackMode=auto + columnGrouping=auto: bars sit next to each other within each category. Easy to compare series within one bucket.

viz_stacked.title: 3. Stacked - cumulative volume per day
viz_stacked.description: stackMode=stacked. Each series on top of the previous. Each column's TOTAL height represents the sum.

viz_stacked100.title: 4. Stacked 100% - share of traffic
viz_stacked100.description: stackMode=stacked100. Each column normalises to 100%. Best for showing how proportions shift between categories. Y-axis shows %.

viz_overlay.title: 5. Overlay grouping - actual on top of target
viz_overlay.description: columnGrouping=overlay: series render IN FRONT of each other (transparent), not side-by-side. Most useful for actual-vs-target patterns where target is a wide pale bar and actual is a narrower bright bar.

viz_negative.title: 6. Negative values - delta with zero baseline
viz_negative.description: showYAxisWithZero=true forces the y-axis to include 0 so positive and negative bars share a common baseline. seriesColorsByField uses two tones - but a real conditional colour requires DOS (Dynamic Options Syntax).

viz_minmax.title: 7. Data labels - minmax pattern
viz_minmax.description: dataValuesDisplay=minmax labels only the minimum and maximum bars. Less noisy than `all`, more informative than `off`. Use when readers need to know the extremes.

viz_rotated.title: 8. Rotated x-labels - long category names
viz_rotated.description: xAxisLabelRotation=-45 turns the category labels diagonally so long names fit. Allowed values are -90/-45/0/45/90. legendTruncation=ellipsisMiddle keeps the middle of long labels readable.

viz_annotations.title: 9. Event annotations - second dataSource
viz_annotations.description: Column charts support the same annotation pattern as line/area: a separate dataSource with annotationX/Label/Color fields. annotationX must match a category value present in the primary search.

viz_dualaxis.title: 10. Dual y-axis - volume column + conversion line
viz_dualaxis.description: overlayFields moves the conversion_pct series to the right axis (line, not column). Most common business chart: volume on left, rate on right.

viz_split.title: 11. Split series - independent y-ranges
viz_split.description: showSplitSeries=true breaks each series into its own sub-chart. showIndependentYRanges=true gives each its own scale - essential when comparing series with very different magnitudes (here cpu_pct, memory_gb, disk_iops).

viz_hidden_chrome.title: 12. Hidden chrome - sparkbar (everything off)
viz_hidden_chrome.description: Sparkbar pattern: all chrome off. The shape is the only signal. Use inside KPI tiles or executive cards where the column chart accompanies a single value.
